text.title: Agentic Product Spec Requirements
text.summary: Required sections and quality gates for an agentic product requirements document.

rule.statement: Every agentic product spec must include: goal state, autonomous actions list, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, escalation triggers, success metrics, and failure modes.
rule.rationale: Specs that describe agentic behavior in natural language ("the agent will handle complex cases") are not implementable. Each autonomous action must be enumerated with its triggering conditions.

list.item: Goal state: machine-verifiable definition of success (how do we know the agent succeeded?).
Autonomous actions: exhaustive list of what the agent may do without user approval.
Human-in-the-loop checkpoints: which actions require approval and why.
Escalation triggers: conditions that pause the agent and alert a human.
Success metrics: goal achievement rate, autonomy ratio, cost-per-task targets.
Failure modes and recovery plan: what the agent does when it hits an edge case.

rule.statement: Write the escalation path before the happy path in the spec.
rule.rationale: The happy path is optimistic and incomplete. The escalation path is what prevents production disasters and enables stakeholder trust in the autonomous system.

antipattern.description: Spec defines the happy path in detail and notes "edge cases to be handled in v2."
antipattern.reason: Edge cases in production are not deferred — they are the failure mode. Without an escalation path, the agent will either freeze or act incorrectly on the cases that matter most.

rule.statement: Any agentic spec in a regulated domain (finance, health, legal) must explicitly enumerate which outputs require human sign-off before action.
rule.rationale: This cannot be left as an implementation detail. Regulatory accountability requires documented human oversight points in the product spec, not the codebase.

rule.statement: Version the agentic product's behavior; when the underlying model changes, run regression tests against the defined goal achievement rate and autonomy ratio thresholds.
rule.rationale: Model providers update models without code releases. Agentic product behavior can change silently. Without regression tests, production incidents are detected by users, not operators.

antipattern.description: Team upgrades to a new model version without running behavioral tests because "the model is better."
antipattern.reason: A better model on benchmark tasks may produce different outputs on the product's specific inputs. Goal achievement rate and autonomy ratio must be validated empirically before rollout.

text.reference: Prompt for drafting an agentic PRD
Prompt for reviewing a spec for gaps
